name: W_CIDeconvolve_benchmark
description: 3D/2D deconvolution for widefield and confocal microscopy images with multiple algorithm backends (sdeconv, CI SHB-RL, pycudadecon, deconwolf, DeconvolutionLab2, redlionfish, scikit-image). Supports optional GPU acceleration via CUDA and OpenCL.

inputs.name: (ess) Deconvolution method
inputs.description: Which deconvolution backend to use.

inputs.name: (ess) RL iterations
inputs.description: Number of iterations to run

inputs.name: (ess) Overrule image metadata
inputs.description: When disabled, image metadata is used where present and descriptor values are used only as fallbacks for missing metadata. When enabled, descriptor values always replace image metadata.

inputs.name: (ess) Numerical aperture
inputs.description: Override objective numerical aperture. Used as a fallback when image metadata is missing, or as an override when image metadata is overruled.

inputs.name: (ess) Emission wavelengths
inputs.description: Override emission wavelengths in nm, comma-separated (one per channel). Used as fallbacks when image metadata is missing, or as overrides when image metadata is overruled. Example: '520, 600'.

inputs.name: (ess) Pixel size XY (nm)
inputs.description: Lateral pixel size in nanometers. Used as a fallback when image metadata is missing, or as an override when image metadata is overruled.

inputs.name: (ess) Pixel size Z (nm)
inputs.description: Axial pixel size / Z step in nanometers. Used as a fallback when image metadata is missing, or as an override when image metadata is overruled.

inputs.name: (ess) Microscope type
inputs.description: Override microscope type for PSF model. Used as a fallback when image metadata is missing, or as an override when image metadata is overruled. Confocal uses emission × excitation PSF when excitation wavelengths are provided.

inputs.name: (ess) Excitation wavelengths
inputs.description: Excitation wavelengths in nm, comma-separated (one per channel). Used for confocal PSF generation (emission × excitation model). Used as fallbacks when image metadata is missing, or as overrides when image metadata is overruled. Example: '405, 488'.

inputs.name: (ess) Confocal pinhole (AU)
inputs.description: Confocal pinhole diameter in Airy Disk units. Used as a fallback when image metadata is missing, or as an override when image metadata is overruled. Use 0 for the legacy point-detector model.

inputs.name: (ess) Immersion medium
inputs.description: Immersion medium refractive index for PSF generation. Used as a fallback when image metadata is missing, or as an override when image metadata is overruled.

inputs.name: (ess) Sample / mounting medium
inputs.description: Refractive index of the sample or mounting medium for PSF generation. Used as a fallback when image metadata is missing, or as an override when image metadata is overruled.

inputs.name: (ess) Z-projection
inputs.description: Output a 2D Z-projection instead of the full 3D stack. 'mip': maximum intensity projection, 'sum': sum projection (converted to float32 if needed), 'none': keep full 3D stack. Ignored for 2D input images.

inputs.name: (ess) Perform Benchmark
inputs.description: If true, run the benchmark suite instead of single deconvolution. The benchmark records performance metrics, writes structured CSV + provenance outputs, and adds no-reference quality metrics.

inputs.name: (ess) Benchmark iterations
inputs.description: Comma-separated list of iteration counts for benchmarking, e.g. '20, 40, 60'. Only used when Perform Benchmark is enabled.

inputs.name: (ess) Benchmark methods
inputs.description: Which set of methods to benchmark. Each tier adds methods to the previous. 'all' includes every public backend. Methods that are unsupported on the current machine are written to the CSV as skipped with a reason.
value-choices: sdeconv_rl, pycudadecon_rl_cuda, ci_rl, ci_rl_tv, ci_sparse_hessian, redlionfish_rl
sdeconv_rl, pycudadecon_rl_cuda, ci_rl, ci_rl_tv, ci_sparse_hessian, deconwolf_rl, deconwolf_shb, redlionfish_rl
sdeconv_wiener, pycudadecon_rl_cuda, ci_rl, ci_rl_tv, ci_sparse_hessian, sdeconv_rl, skimage_cucim_rl, deconvlab2_landweber

inputs.name: (ess) Benchmark crop
inputs.description: If true, centre-crop images to tile-size limits before benchmarking (fast, avoids tiling). If false, process full-size images (may use tiling depending on tiling settings). Only used when Perform Benchmark is enabled.

inputs.name: (ess) Benchmark one image
inputs.description: If true, benchmark only the first image (sorted by name) from the input folder. If false, benchmark all input images. Only used when Perform Benchmark is enabled.

inputs.name: (ess) Benchmark montage
inputs.description: If true, generate visual montage PNGs in addition to the benchmark CSV and provenance JSON. If false, only structured benchmark outputs are written.

inputs.name: (adv) Tiling
inputs.description: XY tiling mode for large images. 'none': disable tiling. 'custom': use tile size limits from the Tile limits parameter.

inputs.name: (adv) Tile limits
inputs.description: Maximum tile dimensions as 'max_xy, max_z'. Only used when Tiling is set to 'custom'. The image is split into the minimum number of XY tiles so each tile fits within these limits.

inputs.name: (adv) Compute device
inputs.description: Force compute device for sdeconv/deconwolf/redlionfish backends. 'auto' uses CUDA if available, otherwise CPU.

inputs.name: (adv) Save console log
inputs.description: If true, save a copy of the console output to a .log file in the output folder.
